title: Converting the Location (country) column to a Parent area
excerpt:
Hi there, 
I’m searching for a solution based on the Location column. 
I want to set a location (for instance country “China”) and it will set automatically a status label “Example”. Anyone who can help with that? 
In ou…

posts.author: Hezibo (Hezi Bosmi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,
I’m searching for a solution based on the Location column.
I want to set a location (for instance country “China”) and it will set automatically a status label “Example”. Anyone who can help with that?
In our workflow, any country has a parent area (not especially continent) so we want that any time someone uses the location column and set a country to fill the parent area automatically.
Thank you

posts.author: Franco (Franco Wiltink)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
i am looking for that to.

posts.author: Franco (Franco Wiltink)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ok, i have a work around for it.
But it will take some time to make the formula for all the country’s

IF({Country} = “Netherlands”, “Europe”, IF({Country} = “United States”, “America North”, “”))

posts.author: Hezibo (Hezi Bosmi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Nice. But the output will be a formula column which you can’t perform automation based on it.
But we may use another app to copy the formula to status label.
Thx

posts.author: Franco (Franco Wiltink)
posts.cookedHtml:
App like general caster will do the trick i guess.
I simplefied the formula a bit.

IF(OR({Country}=“Netherlands”,{Country}=“Belgium”,{Country}=“Luxembourg”,{Country}=“Poland”,{Country}=“Italy”,{Country}=“United Kingdom”,{Country}=“France”,{Country}=“Turkey”,{Country}=“Denmark”,{Country}=“Sweden”),“Europe”,“”)
